messages.content:
Which of the following statements is correct? (Select one (1) alternative)

UNICEF operational aspects of a programme focus on activities and their immediate results to demostate its contribution to its stakeholders.
RRBM it is only used in country offices during the 5 years country programme cycle.
RRBM iterative process is; defining activities; test our hypotheses; monitor our implementation; learn; and adapt/adjust our interventions along the way as needed to ensure the desired result is met.
A result is a describable or measurable change in a state or condition that derives from a cause and effect relationship.
A result is a describable or measurable change in a state or condition that derives from a cause and effect relationship.

Which of the following statements defines correctly the Rights and Results-Based Management approach? (Select one (1) alternative)

RRBM helps to achieve the results at any cost.
RRBM involves a shift in focus from what we are doing, the activities, to the changes we want to make in a short time, the outputs.
RRBM involves a shift in focus from what we are doing, the activities, to the changes we want to make or to which we want to contribute, the results.
RRBM supports all the processes during the country programme cycle to achieve activities that last longer.
RRBM involves a shift in focus from what we are doing, the activities, to the changes we want to make or to which we want to contribute, the results.

The relationship between results achieve as an effect of your activities and the subsequent results and impact it's called...(Select one (1) alternative)

Outcomes/Impact achieved
Results chain
Results relation
Outcome achieved
Results chain

n order to adopt a rights- and results-based culture effectively... (Select one (1) alternative)

We need to ensure that all UNICEF staff subscribe to the respect and integity UNICEF core values, since the other core values are not so relevant to the country programme cycle.
RRBM has to be applied only throughout the 2 firsts years of the country programme cycle ensuring that all UNICEF staff subscribe to the respect and integity UNICEF core values, since the other core values are not so relevant to the country programme cycle.
We need to ensure that all UNICEF staff subscribe to UNICEF core values, which are closely aligned with the RRBM principles.
RRBM has to be applied only throughout the 2 firsts years of the country programme cycle.
We need to ensure that all UNICEF staff subscribe to UNICEF core values, which are closely aligned with the RRBM principles.

For UNICEF the core value respesct means that...(Select one (1) alternative)

programmes can only be effective if the recipients of our support feel understood and respected, and if we pay our colleagues at all levels the same respect that we want for ourselves.
programmes care for, and support, children, young people and mothers around the world, in every context.
when delivery programmes or services, we are acting with the best interests at heart for the communities, and we trust one another to do the right thing at all times.
public and private donors, as well as children and vulnerable people worldwide, deserve to know how UNICEF’s programmes are run, how resources are spent, and how results are being achieved.
programmes can only be effective if the recipients of our support feel understood and respected, and if we pay our colleagues at all levels the same respect that we want for ourselves.

The purposes of RRBM are: (Select all that apply)

Communicating results
Informing decision making
Learning from what we do
Stakeholder engagement
Broad-based application

The purposes of RRBM are:

1. Communicating results
2. Informing decision making
3. Learning from what we do
4. Stakeholder engagement

A rights and results culture means that...(Select all that apply)

It requires only procedures and tools along with an accountability framework that is informed by both respect and integrity Core Values.
Staff act as knowledge workers who engage in critical inquiry and transformative learning.
It is linked to an accountability framework and is informed only by both respect and integrity Core Values.
It requires not only procedures and tools but also changes in mindsets and ways of doing business for all staff in the organization.
It emphasizes change management and the “people side” of change to empower all staff to enhance the capacity of the organization to innovate in order to achieve results.

A rights and results culture means that:

1. Staff act as knowledge workers who engage in critical inquiry and transformative learning.
2. It requires not only procedures and tools but also changes in mindsets and ways of doing business for all staff in the organization.
3. It emphasizes change management and the “people side” of change to empower all staff to enhance the capacity of the organization to innovate in order to achieve results.

For UNICEF , Strategic Planning with an RRBM approach in general includes among others: (Select all that apply)

Prepare the results-based reporting format.
Prioritizing issues to be addressed by UNICEF action.
Developing a Strategic Plan with a results framework
Managing and mitigating risks.
Understanding the situation, particulary the causes of a problem.

For UNICEF, Strategic Planning with an RRBM approach generally includes:

1. Prioritizing issues to be addressed by UNICEF action.
2. Developing a Strategic Plan with a results framework.
3. Managing and mitigating risks.
4. Understanding the situation, particularly the causes of a problem.

Select the RRBM principle that matches with this description: RRBM aims to keep our programming frameworks and tools as simple as possible. Select one (1) alternative.

Broad-based application
Chil rigths at the center
Simplicity
Stakeholder engagement
Simplicity

Which of the following alternatives correspond to the right order the RRBM iterative process?

1. Defining Results & How to get there, 2. Monitor implementation, 3. Adapt/adjust interventions along the way as needed, 4. Learn, 5. Test hypotheses.
1. Test hypotheses, 2. Defining Results & How to get there, 3. Monitor implementation, 4. Learn, 5. Adapt/adjust interventions along the way as needed.
1. Defining Results & How to get there, 2. Monitor implementation, 3. Learn, 4. Test hypotheses, 5. Adapt/adjust interventions along the way as needed.
1. Defining Results & How to get there, 2. Test hypotheses, 3. Monitor implementation, 4. Learn, 5. Adapt/adjust interventions along the way as needed.
Finish

The correct order for the RRBM iterative process is:

1. Defining Results & How to get there
2. Test hypotheses
3. Monitor implementation
4. Learn
5. Adapt/adjust interventions along the way as needed

Drag the following concepts into the correct boxes.

 - is the ability of children, communities, and systems to anticipate, prevent, withstand, adapt to, and recover from stresses and shocks.

 - is about setting clear goals and targets, being responsible for delivering on them and accepting potential sanctions for lack of compliance with commitments.

 - focuses on meeting the needs of the present generation without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their needs.

Resilience: is the ability of children, communities, and systems to anticipate, prevent, withstand, adapt to, and recover from stresses and shocks.

Accountability: is about setting clear goals and targets, being responsible for delivering on them and accepting potential sanctions for lack of compliance with commitments.

Sustainability: focuses on meeting the needs of the present generation without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their needs.

The main human rights treaties for UNICEF's work are (select all that apply):

CRPD
CEDAW and CCRC
The American Convention on Human Rights and the European Convention on Human Rights.
African Charter on Human and Peoples' Rights
International Humanitarian Law and Refugee Law

The main human rights treaties for UNICEF's work are:

- CRPD (Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities)
- CEDAW (Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women) and CCRC (Optional Protocol to the Convention on the Rights of the Child)
- International Humanitarian Law and Refugee Law (not specific treaties, but legal frameworks related to humanitarian and refugee rights)

Which of the following alternatives corresponds to the guiding principles of Convention on the Rights of the Child? Select one (1) alternative.

  1. Non-discrimination, 2. Best interests of the child, 3. Rights to life, survival and development, 4. Respect for the views of the child.
1. Identifying and addressing inequalities, 2. Best interests of the child, 3. Rights to life, survival and development, 4. Respect for the views of the child.
1. Non-discrimination, 2. Best interests of the child, 3. Rights to life, survival and development, 4. Quality services and protection of the child
1. Non-discrimination, 2. Best interests of the child, 3. Reduce disparities, 4. Respect for the views of the child.

The correct answer is:

1. Non-discrimination
2. Best interests of the child
3. Rights to life, survival and development
4. Respect for the views of the child

Which step of the "Leave no one behind" principle 5-step approach does the text below correspond to? Prioritization and analysis involve identifying what are the immediate, underlying and root causes of the deprivations, disadvantages and discriminations. Select one (1) alternative.

1. Who is left behind?
5. Who has accountability?
4. How should progress be measured and monitored?
2. Why? What rights are at stake?
The text corresponds to Step 2: Why? What rights are at stake?

What are the humanitarian principles as defined by international humanitarian law? Select one (1) alternative

Humanity, Neutrality, self-subsistence
Humanity, Neutrality, Impartiality, Independence
Humanity, Neutrality, Impartiality, self-subsistence
Neutrality, Impartiality, Independence
The correct answer is: Humanity, Neutrality, Impartiality, Independence

Which of the following alternatives are correct about CCC? (Select all that apply)

It’s intended only to UNICEF staff and civil society organizations.
Does not include explicit strategies to link humanitarian and development action.
Forms the core UNICEF policy & framework for humanitarian action
It's grounded in global & humanitarian norm
Sets organizational, programmatic, and operational commitments and benchmarks against which UNICEF holds itself accountable

The correct alternatives are:

- Forms the core UNICEF policy & framework for humanitarian action.
- It's grounded in global & humanitarian norm.
- Sets organizational, programmatic, and operational commitments and benchmarks against which UNICEF holds itself accountable.

Which of the following alternatives correspond to the human-rights disability model? (Select all that apply)

Aims to remove attitudinal, environmental, and institutional barriers to social inclusion.
Disability is recognized as part of the diversity that makes up every society.
Decisions are made by medical experts.
Appeals to pity and generosity rather than rights.
It focuses on inclusion, empowerment and autonomy, to ensure that people with disabilities participate in and benefit from programmes.

The alternatives that correspond to the human-rights disability model are:

- Aims to remove attitudinal, environmental, and institutional barriers to social inclusion.
- Disability is recognized as part of the diversity that makes up every society.
- It focuses on inclusion, empowerment and autonomy, to ensure that people with disabilities participate in and benefit from programmes.

Which of the following alternatives correspond to the human-rights disability model? (Select all that apply)

Aims to remove attitudinal, environmental, and institutional barriers to social inclusion.
Disability is recognized as part of the diversity that makes up every society.
Decisions are made by medical experts.
Appeals to pity and generosity rather than rights.
It focuses on inclusion, empowerment and autonomy, to ensure that people with disabilities participate in and benefit from programmes.

The alternatives that correspond to the human-rights disability model are:

- Aims to remove attitudinal, environmental, and institutional barriers to social inclusion.
- Disability is recognized as part of the diversity that makes up every society.
- It focuses on inclusion, empowerment and autonomy, to ensure that people with disabilities participate in and benefit from programmes.

Which of the following alternatives are some of the gender bottlenecks and barriers girls and women are facing? (Select all that apply)

Lack of resources and decision making
Being treated more strictly at school and expected to perform better in specific academic disciplines
Being required to decide what the best is for the family
Damaging and detrimental masculine and feminine ideals and expectations
Lack of safety and mobility

The alternatives that are some of the gender bottlenecks and barriers girls and women are facing are:

- Lack of resources and decision making
- Being treated more strictly at school and expected to perform better in specific academic disciplines
- Damaging and detrimental masculine and feminine ideals and expectations
- Lack of safety and mobility
